_comment: Kona Site Defender WAF configuration for BAZSPARK. This template defines attack groups, rule actions, and custom rules. Apply via /papi/v1/wafconfigs POST. See docs/AKAMAI_DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md.
configDescription: Safety-critical Fire Protection platform — strict WAF policy for engineering API.

attackGroups.comments: Block SQL injection — FireAI uses psycopg2 with parameterized queries, but WAF is defense-in-depth.
Block Cross-Site Scripting — frontend renders engineering data that could be exploited.
Block Local File Inclusion — file upload endpoints parse IFC/DXF/CAD files.
Block Remote Code Execution — critical for engineering API.
Block PHP injection — backend is Python but RCE payloads often probe PHP.
Alert on vulnerability scanners — investigate before blocking (could be pen-test).
Alert on bot signatures — Bot Manager handles this more granularly.

customRules.name: Block direct origin access (HF Space URL leak)
customRules.description: If someone discovers the HF Space direct URL (ahmdelbaz28-bazspark.hf.space) and tries to bypass Akamai, block them. The HF Space URL should never appear in a Referer or Host header from a legitimate client.
customRules.comments: Defense-in-depth: backend/akamai_middleware.py also checks this via the AKAMAI_REQUIRE_ORIGIN_TOKEN env var.

customRules.name: Rate limit /api/v1/auth/login (credential stuffing protection)
customRules.description: Limit login attempts to 10 per minute per IP. Bot Manager + Account Protector also engage, but this rate limit catches missed traffic.
customRules.comments: Backend slowapi limiter also enforces this at app level — defense in depth.

customRules.name: Rate limit /api/v1/* (general API flood protection)
customRules.description: Limit all API calls to 300 per minute per IP. Frontend uses 1-10 calls per page typically.

customRules.name: Block requests without User-Agent
customRules.description: Most legitimate browsers and HTTP clients include a User-Agent header. Missing UA often indicates scripted attack.
customRules.comments: Exceptions: curl with -A '', but legitimate users use curl with explicit UA.

customRules.name: Block requests with suspicious User-Agent (sqlmap, nikto, masscan, etc.)
customRules.description: Common attack tool signatures in User-Agent header.
customRules.condition: OR(http.header.user-agent contains "sqlmap", http.header.user-agent contains "nikto", http.header.user-agent contains "masscan", http.header.user-agent contains "nmap", http.header.user-agent contains "wpscan", http.header.user-agent contains "dirbuster", http.header.user-agent contains "gobuster", http.header.user-agent contains "hydra", http.header.user-agent contains "metasploit")

customRules.name: Block HTTP methods other than GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD/PATCH
customRules.description: Block TRACE/CONNECT and other unusual methods.

customRules.name: Allow ONLY valid Content-Type on POST/PUT/PATCH
customRules.description: Block non-JSON content types on API mutation endpoints.
customRules.condition: AND(http.method in ["POST", "PUT", "PATCH"], NOT(http.header.content-type starts with "application/json"), NOT(http.header.content-type starts with "multipart/form-data"))
customRules.comments: multipart/form-data allowed for file uploads (IFC/DXF parsers).

customRules.name: Block oversized request bodies (>50MB)
customRules.description: FireAI largest legitimate upload is IFC files (~10-20MB). 50MB gives headroom while blocking DoS.

customRules.name: Geo-block sanctioned countries
customRules.description: Block requests from US-sanctioned countries (OFAC list). Backend also enforces via AKAMAI_BLOCKED_COUNTRIES env var.
customRules.comments: Update sanctioned countries list quarterly per OFAC updates.

exceptions.name: Health check endpoint
exceptions.description: Allow /api/health without rate limiting — used by uptime monitors (HF Space healthcheck, Vercel cron, Akamai SureRoute).
exceptions.comments: Without this exception, uptime monitors would be rate-limited.

exceptions.name: Akamai monitoring IPs
exceptions.description: Allow Akamai's monitoring IPs without rate limiting.
exceptions.comments: Akamai edge IP ranges — fetch updated list from /papi/v1/ips.
